understanding.goal_understanding: The objective in each level was to end with every goal tile marked '+' occupied by a selectable piece at the same time. A level is only completed when all goal tiles are covered simultaneously; completing all levels ultimately wins the whole game run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were issued primarily as CLICK on a target grid coordinate [x, y]. A CLICK would attempt to move the currently selected piece to the clicked location if a valid movement/path existed; otherwise the piece would remain in place and still consume a step (as seen by repeated blocked clicks at [36, 18]). Movement was constrained by obstacles/walls that block motion. Selection was persistent (the same piece stayed selected between turns unless explicitly changed), and pieces could be repositioned by clicking different reachable tiles. Pushing, when applicable in the game, would involve moving into an occupied tile to shove a piece if the space beyond was free; if not free, the push/move would be blocked similarly to walls/obstacles.

history.summary: Level 1 attempt 1/2 failed after 64 turns. Restarting the same level.
Level 1 attempt 2/2 failed after 64 turns. No attempts remaining.
